rationale_for_baseline_tier: An infected deep neuropathic diabetic foot ulcer is a limb-threatening emergency requiring urgent same-day in-person High-Risk Foot Service/ED assessment (probe-to-bone, imaging, antibiotics, offloading). A telehealth provider cannot probe, debride or image. Escalate to T5 for sepsis or gangrene.

escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day in-person High-Risk Foot Service/ED assessment today
escalation_edges.rationale: Limb-threatening infection needing urgent multidisciplinary assessment, antibiotics and offloading.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This wound needs to be seen in person today at a hospital or high-risk foot clinic — it's a serious infection even though it doesn't hurt. Keep all weight off it and don't walk barefoot.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever, chills, confusion, redness spreading up the foot/leg, or blackening of the foot
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates sepsis or gangrene — an emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads up the foot or leg, the foot turns black, or he develops a fever, chills or confusion — call 000 for an ambulance immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Poorly controlled diabetes with neuropathy — blunted pain/fever
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Do not rely on pain/fever to gauge severity; escalate on the wound findings and involve the daughter in monitoring.
Enlist the daughter for twice-daily foot checks, offloading adherence and prompt escalation.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This needs urgent in-person assessment today at a hospital or high-risk foot clinic. Keep all weight off the foot, never walk barefoot, take the antibiotics, and have your daughter check the foot twice a day.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day High-Risk Foot Service/ED; 000 for sepsis or a blackening foot
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend ED tonight if a same-day foot service isn't available; 000 for the emergency features.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document an infected neuropathic diabetic foot ulcer, offloading and antibiotics commenced, and urgent High-Risk Foot Service referral with osteomyelitis evaluation.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Home dressing/antibiotics with routine follow-up, reassured by painlessness/absence of fever.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The infection extends to bone/deep spaces; the patient keeps weight-bearing on an insensate foot and loses the limb (or develops sepsis).

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Urgent GP only, without High-Risk Foot Service/ED assessment or osteomyelitis evaluation.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance is appropriate if sepsis/gangrene; otherwise same-day in-person assessment (T4) suffices — minimal over-triage concern.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot probe the wound, assess depth/bone involvement or image remotely; pain/fever are unreliable in neuropathy/diabetes.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as a limb-threatening infected diabetic foot ulcer and escalate to urgent in-person High-Risk Foot/ED assessment with offloading and antibiotics.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Being reassured by painlessness/absence of fever and advising home dressing with routine follow-up.
